title: Seeds and sterility
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 28, 2014 12:37 am Monk Ed wrote: And in that alternate universe where Kaworu didn't use the choker to sacrifice himself to avert 4I, 4I happened and everyone died.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 28, 2014 12:51 am amitakartok wrote: They gotta have, seeing as they wouldn't be able to maintain the illusion of normalcy if they didn't have. Shinji has seen both of them naked on-screen and didn't notice anything unusual, considering his lack of reaction in that regard.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 28, 2014 1:20 am Reichu wrote: I just assume they're both sterile. (I don't have solid proof or anything, but it just... feels right. Plus it dashes the dreams of so many shippers.)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 28, 2014 1:28 am If you do not bleed, releasing eggs you are most certainly not.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sun Sep 28, 2014 2:25 am Reichu wrote: As confusing as the term "fem-Kaworu" is, it actually isn't the same thing as Tabby-chan. At least, not the fem-Kaworu that Brik's sig is talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 28, 2014 4:51 am Reichu wrote: I just assume they're both sterile. (I don't have solid proof or anything, but it just... feels right. Plus it dashes the dreams of so many shippers.)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Sep 28, 2014 6:32 am Reichu wrote: I'm sure he could have scrounged up a less symbolically-charged way to kill himself in a pinch.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Sep 28, 2014 7:07 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: If you do not bleed, releasing eggs you are most certainly not.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 28, 2014 7:09 am Rei IV wrote: I know, that's why I'm referring to Tabby-chan. You know, an actual female incarnation of our beloved Bishonen Space Jesus. Why have that monstrosity (FemKaworu) when you have an actual female Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Sep 28, 2014 7:58 am NemZ wrote: On the contrary, that whole bleeding business happens when women STOP being fertile. So If Rei is a 'woman that never bleeds' it's possible that she's constantly fertile. Seems fitting for the whole seed of life thing, no?

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:04 am NemZ wrote: So If Rei is a 'woman that never bleeds' it's possible that she's constantly fertile.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:05 am I always thought that no periods meant no eggs to release, therefore no fertility. It could be symbolic of always being fertile within terms of the whole “seed of life” thing while establishing the reality that Rei will never have any human children, though. I can see that as a neat interpretation. But “ woman who never bleeds” (note: not “being/creature/life form who never bleeds,” which indicates that she’s speaking of her human self) has a very clear meaning to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:06 am amitakartok wrote: Rei's genetic alterations might have accidentally caused her to "revert to the earlier revision", so to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:07 am ^^ She's can't be TOO different in that regard, considering that she still has enough hormones to develop a very much feminine figure. Therefore, she must have ovaries at the very least.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 28, 2014 9:06 am ElMariachi wrote: Do you know how periods works?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Sep 28, 2014 9:23 am I'm not sure I see how that's unambiguous. In fact it's VERY ambiguous... we aren't even 100% sure that Rei is talking about herself there, for starters. Besides, if the show can call angels 'human' despite some pretty glaring biological differences then we can be at least a tiny bit flexible when it uses the word 'woman' to not limit this to strictly standard issue homo sapien females? Hell, even the word choice of woman (assuming this isn't a translation issue) suggests it refers to someone who is fertile rather than still just a girl. My point is there's a lot of wiggle room there. ElMariachi wrote: Do you know how periods works?

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Sep 28, 2014 9:41 am Bagheera wrote: Of course, Asuka doesn't have this problem. She's all too painfully aware of being a fully functional woman!

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sun Sep 28, 2014 10:56 am Reichu wrote: Fem-Kaworu is female, though, just with the secondary sexual characteristics of a human male. The origin of f-K is that I convinced myself while hypomanic, i.e. temporarily insane, that the Kaworu we see onscreen is secretly a girl. (I still find the concept rather compelling and assume I'll find a good use for it eventually.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 28, 2014 11:45 am Bagheera: Think about what Rei actually is, a false container for making Lilith, a veritable force of nature, a bit more controllable. That it would be sterile makes perfect sense to me, as it adds to the "falseness" of her existence that Rei intuitively senses (i.e., the mother of all, trapped in a body that can never mother). Rei IV wrote: Reichu, I'm noticing that since your return, there's been a lot more active discussion that's resulted in multiple splits lately. I can't help but consider that a good sign.

[]:             Quote Post by JonnyNova » Sun Sep 28, 2014 11:58 am Couldn't it also said that Rei's body is stolen as well? It is a cheap copy of Yui's body.
summary:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 28, 2014 12:37 am Monk Ed wrote: And in that alternate universe where Kaworu didn't use the choker to sacrifice himself to avert 4I, 4I happened and everyone died.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 28, 2014 12:51 am amitakartok wrote: They gotta have, seeing as they wouldn't be able to maintain the illusion
